title: Warm Jamaican Coconut Pasta
description: An earthy, fluffy, nostalgic Jamaican dish featuring jam n ib rico, a pasta-water emulsion, and an egg‑free-friendly sauce.
instructions: Boil a large pot of salted water and cook the pasta until al dente, about 8 to 10 minutes.
While the pasta cooks, heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and sauté the diced onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the dried thyme and allspice, cooking for another minute to bloom the spices.
Pour in the jam n ib rico and coconut milk, whisking to combine and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Once the sauce thickens slightly, add the cooked pasta directly into the skillet, tossing to coat the noodles evenly.
If the pasta seems dry, add a splash of the reserved pasta cooking water to achieve a silky emulsion.
Season the dish with salt and black pepper to taste, then remove from heat.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped cilantro if desired.
